column1: The first header refers to the abbreviation of the treatment (positive control, negative control, and CPAs), and each treatment has its own section. For binary or ternary solutions, the total concentration is equally divided between each CPA on a molal basis.
In each treatment section, an experiment is identified by the max treatment concentration [molal] and the target exposure time [min] for that concentration (first two columns of information).
After the identification of the experiment and moving to the next column, the average temperature [oC] at which the experiment was conducted is listed.

The next two columns list the cell viabilities. Viabilities are normalized to the positive control and there are either 5 or 6 replicates per experimental condition. Both the cell viability and adjusted cell viability are listed. If a cell viability is shaded red, it has been identified as an outlier
Continuing across the columns, the specifics of each treatment step are described with two headers:

column2: Number headers refer to the step of the addition and removal procedure.
Letter headers refer to a piece of information for that given step as described below:
